info.title: Cella DEVELOPMENT API
info.description:
⚠️ ATTENTION: PRERELEASE!
                  This API is organized into modules based on logical domains.

x-extensions.description: Authorization middleware applied to the endpoint

publicGuard.name: public
publicGuard.description: No authentication required; provides baseDb without RLS context

authGuard.name: auth
authGuard.description: Requires valid session and sets auth context (user, memberships, baseDb)

crossTenantGuard.name: crossTenant
crossTenantGuard.description: Requires authGuard and sets baseDb for cross-tenant access

orgGuard.name: org
orgGuard.description: Validates organization membership within tenant context

relatableGuard.name: relatable
relatableGuard.description: Checks that the requesting user shares at least one organization with the target user

tenantGuard.name: tenant
tenantGuard.description: Requires authGuard, validates tenant access, and sets baseDb + tenantId context

x-extensions.description: Rate limiting rules applied to the endpoint

spamLimiter.name: spam
spamLimiter.description: Max 10 requests/hour per user (per IP when anonymous) for email-sending endpoints

emailEnumLimiter.name: emailEnum
emailEnumLimiter.description: Blocks IP for 30 min after 5 consecutive failures

presignedUrlLimiter.name: presignedUrl
presignedUrlLimiter.description: Max 2000 requests/hour per user for presigned URLs

totpVerificationLimiter.name: totpVerification
totpVerificationLimiter.description: Blocks IP for 30 min after 5 failed TOTP attempts

magicLinkLimiter.name: magicLink
magicLinkLimiter.description: Max 2 magic link emails per 30 min per email address

pointsLimiter.name: points
pointsLimiter.description: Consumes 1 API point(s) per request against per-tenant hourly budget

tokenLimiter.name: token
tokenLimiter.description: Blocks IP for 30 min after 10 consecutive token failures

insertEntityLock.name: insertEntityLock
insertEntityLock.description: Prevents concurrent entity creation for the same tenant

x-extensions.description: Caching strategy applied to the endpoint

appCache.name: app
appCache.description: Entity-keyed detail cache with per-request read authorization

x-extensions.description: Tool registration metadata for AI and MCP integrations
Service gating the endpoint; route returns 404 when the service is disabled

tags.name: cella
tags.description: Core modules provided by cella.

tags.name: app
tags.description: Application-specific modules.

tags.name: data
tags.description: Complete data schemas

tags.name: base
tags.description: Schemas with base fields only

tags.name: errors
tags.description: Error schemas

tags.name: channel
tags.description: Operations on channel entities (entities that have memberships, e.g. organizations, projects).

tags.name: product
tags.description: Operations on product entities (content entities without membership, e.g. tasks, pages, attachments).

tags.name: attachments
tags.description:
Endpoints for managing file based attachments (such as images, PDFs, and documents) linked to
    entities such as organizations or users. Files are uploaded directly by the client, while the API handles
    metadata registration, linking, access, and preview utilities.

tags.name: auth
tags.description:
Endpoints for authentication, supporting multiple sign-in methods including OAuth
    (Google, Microsoft, GitHub) and passkeys (WebAuthn). They cover sign-up, sign-in, email verification,
    account linking, and impersonation for system admins.

tags.name: entities
tags.description:
Endpoints that operate across multiple entity types, such as users and organizations.
    Entities are identifiable domain objects that may be contextual, hierarchical (with parent/child
    relations), or actor-like. These routes provide shared logic across modules, including slug validation
    and entity visibility.

tags.name: mcp
tags.description:
Endpoints exposing the AI capability layer, which is a server-side tool registry surfaced to
    external clients through a Model Context Protocol (MCP) endpoint. It ships no LLM transport or agent of its
    own; forks build AI features and agent products (such as chat) on top, reusing the same tool registry.

tags.name: me
tags.description:
Endpoints scoped to the current user, meaning the user associated with the active session making
    the request. Unlike the users endpoints, which may operate on any user in the system, me endpoints act
    exclusively on the current user and follow a different authorization flow.

tags.name: memberships
tags.description:
Endpoints for managing memberships, which represent one-to-one relationships between a
    user and a contextual entity such as an organization. Each membership includes role information and
    status flags such as archived or muted. Memberships can also reference parent entities to easily have a
    hierarchical context available.

tags.name: metrics
tags.description: Endpoints for retrieving high-level counts for entities such as users and organizations.

tags.name: organizations
tags.description:
Endpoints for managing organizations, which are core channel entities and the highest ancestor
    in the entity hierarchy. Organizations define access boundaries and are typically the primary scope for
    permissions and resource management.

tags.name: requests
tags.description:
Endpoints for handling incoming requests such as contact form submissions, newsletter signups,
    and waitlist entries.

tags.name: seen
tags.description: Endpoints for tracking entity view counts and marking entities as seen by the current user.

tags.name: system
tags.description:
System level endpoints for administrative actions and platform wide functionality, such as user
    invitations, file uploads, and webhook handling.

tags.name: tenants
tags.description:
Endpoints for managing tenants, which are top-level isolation boundaries used by Row-Level
    Security (RLS) to partition data. Only system administrators can manage tenants.

tags.name: users
tags.description:
Endpoints for managing users at the system level. Unlike channel entities such as
    organizations, a user is a global entity that is not scoped to a specific context. These endpoints are
    intended for administrative operations on any user in the system.

tags.name: yjs
tags.description:
Endpoints for Yjs collaborative editing support. They provide auth tokens for the Yjs relay worker
    and accept client-computed derived fields from collaborative editing sessions.

cookieAuth.name: cella-development-session-v2
cookieAuth.description: Authentication cookie. Copy cookie from your network tab and paste it here. If you don't have it, you need to sign in or sign up first.

NotFoundError.description: Not found: resource does not exist.

UserBase.description: Base user schema with essential fields for identification and display.

ChannelEntityBase.description: Base schema for entities with memberships (e.g. organization).

ProductEntityBase.description: Base schema for content entities with creator tracking (e.g. page, attachment).

example.name: Capitulus vesco cursim alter.
example.description: Veritas itaque basium a. Sufficio ter nostrum cilicium tersus ara. Earum ascit trans somniculosus.

MembershipBase.description: Core membership fields shared across active and inactive memberships.

fieldTimestamps.description: Per-field HLC timestamps for scalar fields being changed

StxBase.description: Sync transaction metadata for offline and realtime support, idempotency and HLC-based conflict resolution.

kind.description: Discriminant for the notification: product-entity sync vs membership change

action.description: Change kind; moveOut = the row left this path (reparent) and is no longer readable there

channelType.description: Channel entity type for membership events (e.g. organization, project)

path.description: Materialized id-path of the affected rows (root-first ancestor ids); moveOut carries the OLD path

seq.description: Org-sequence position (one order per organization, shared across product entity types)

channelId.description: Channel entity ID for grouping (e.g. projectId for tasks in unseen counts)

allOf.description: Sync transaction metadata for HLC conflict resolution

batchUntilSeq.description: Last sequence position for a batched notification — client should fetch range

count.description: Authoritative row count for batches: sequence ranges of different paths may interleave

syncWindow.description: Server-suggested spread window (ms) for the lazy delta fetch — scales with channel audience and load; the client clamps it between its eagerness tier bounds

sourceType.description: Entity type that triggered the propagation (e.g. label)

targetType.description: Entity type whose cache should be invalidated (e.g. task)

field.description: Field on the target entity that references the source (e.g. labels)

update.description: Target entity IDs that need cache refresh

remove.description: Target entity IDs that need the source reference removed

propagation.description: Embedded entity propagation hint for cross-entity cache invalidation

StreamNotification.description: Realtime notification delivered via SSE for entity and membership changes.

BadRequestError.description: Error returned when the request is malformed or contains invalid data.

ApiError.description: Standard error response returned by all API endpoints.

UnauthorizedError.description: Error returned when authentication is missing or invalid.

ForbiddenError.description: Error returned when the user lacks permission for the requested action.

NotFoundError.description: Error returned when the requested resource cannot be found.

ConflictError.description: Error returned when the request conflicts with current resource state.

TooManyRequestsError.description: Error returned when rate limits are exceeded.

isSystemAdmin.description: Whether the current user has system admin privileges.

Me.description: The currently authenticated user with their system admin status.

User.description: A user with profile data and activity timestamps.

MeAuthData.description: Authentication metadata for the current user session.

InactiveMembership.description: A membership record for a user who has not yet accepted an invitation.

UploadToken.description: A signed token authorizing file uploads to the configured storage provider.

apiPointsPerHour.description: Max API points per hour per user within this tenant (0 = no tenant limit; the global safety ceiling still applies)

domainsCount.description: Number of domains claimed by this tenant

Tenant.description: A tenant representing an isolated data partition for multi-tenancy.

Request.description: A contact or waitlist submission from an unauthenticated user.

Organization.description: The main channel entity is an organization.

example.name: Spencer, Pouros and Bergnaum
example.welcomeText: Welcome to Spencer, Pouros and Bergnaum!

Attachment.description: A product entity for file attachment metadata.

Membership.description: A user's membership in a channel entity, including role and activity data.

get.description: Returns auth health status including whether the client IP is rate-limited for email enumeration protection.

post.description: Checks if a user with the specified email address exists in the system.

get.description: Validates and invokes a token (for email verification, invitations, mfa) and redirects user to backend with a one-purpose, single-use token session in a cookie.
Get basic token data from single-use token session, It returns basic data if the session is still valid.
Allows a system admin to impersonate a specific user by ID, returning a temporary impersonation session.
Ends impersonation by clearing the current impersonation session and restoring the admin context.

post.description: Resends an invitation email with token to a new user using the provided email address and token ID.
Signs out the current user and clears the active session.
Sends a magic link sign-in email to the specified address. Always returns 204 to prevent email enumeration.

204.description: Magic link email sent (or silently ignored if email not found)

post.description: Generates a new TOTP key for current user and returns a provisioning URI and Base32 manual key.
Confirms TOTP setup by verifying a code from the authenticator app for the first time. On success, TOTP is registered for current user.

delete.description: Delete TOTP credential for current user.

post.description: Validates the TOTP code and completes TOTP based authentication.
Register a passkey for passwordless authentication by verifying a signed challenge and linking it to the current user. Multiple passkeys can be created for different devices/browsers.

example.userAgent: Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 11; SM-G998B) AppleWebKit/576.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/108.7.7.15 Mobile Safari/574.19

delete.description: Delete a passkey by id from the current user.

post.description: Initiates the passkey registration or authentication flow by generating a device bound challenge.
Validates the signed challenge and completes passkey based authentication.

get.description: Starts OAuth authentication with GitHub. Can be used for account connection, email verification, invitation process, defaults to authentication.
Starts OAuth authentication with Google. Can be used for account connection, email verification, invitation process, defaults to authentication.
Starts OAuth authentication with Microsoft. Can be used for account connection, email verification, invitation process, defaults to authentication.
Handles GitHub OAuth callback, retrieves user identity, and establishes a session or links account.
Handles Google OAuth callback, retrieves user identity, and establishes a session or links account.
Handles Microsoft OAuth callback, retrieves user identity, and establishes a session or links account.
Returns the current user.

delete.description: Deletes the current user. This also removes the user's memberships (cascade) and sets references to the user to null where applicable.

put.description: Enable or disable multifactor authentication for the current user. Always requires passkey or TOTP reauthentication.

get.description: Returns authentication related data of current user, including sessions, OAuth accounts, and sign in options.
Returns a list of pending memberships with entity data.

delete.description: Ends one or more sessions for the current user based on provided session IDs.

rejectedIds.description: Identifiers of items that could not be processed

rejectionReasons.description: Map of reason code to rejected item IDs

delete.description: Removes the current user from an entity they are a member of.

get.description: Generates and returns an upload token for uploading files or images to a private S3 bucket, scoped to the current user and organization

200.description: Upload token with a scope for a user or organization

get.description: Unsubscribes the user from email notifications using a personal unsubscribe token. No authentication is required, as the token implicitly identifies the current user.
Returns all memberships for the current user across all channel entities.

Returns the number of unseen product entities per parent channel entity (e.g., project) and entity type for the current user. Computed within the rolling seen window so entities older than seen_by retention do not participate.

200.description: Unseen counts per parent channel entity per entity type

post.description:
Checks whether a given slug is available within a tenant for the specified entity type.
      Primarily used to prevent slug collisions before creating or updating an entity.

get.description: SSE stream for membership and entity notifications affecting the current user. Sends lightweight notifications.

post.description: Fetch missed entity and membership changes since last sync. Send cursor and declared views (prefix sets + org-sequence cursors) in the body.

cursor.description: Last activity cursor received by the client (LSN-based). Omit on first sync.

key.description: Client-chosen stable view key, echoed back verbatim to correlate responses

prefixes.description: Materialized id-path prefixes this view covers (root-first ids, slash-joined)

depth.description: View depth: subtree (default) covers rows at or below the prefix node; self covers only rows HOMED at the node (exact placement — a channel wall). Self views are answerable by direct home-scoped memberships.

cursor.description: Org-sequence position this view has fully ingested (0 = baseline not yet established)

views.description: Client-declared views: prefix set + entity types + org-sequence cursor per view

sourceType.description: Entity type that triggered the propagation (e.g. label)

targetType.description: Entity type whose cache should be invalidated (e.g. task)

field.description: Field on the target entity that references the source (e.g. labels)

update.description: Target entity IDs that need cache refresh

remove.description: Target entity IDs that need the source reference removed

key.description: The client-supplied view key, echoed verbatim

frontiers.description: Per-entityType newest sequence position over the view prefixes (subtree: f:{type}; self: fs:{type})

counts.description: Per-entityType live row counts summed over the view prefixes (subtree: e:{type}; self: es:{type})

views.description: Per-view answers for client-declared views (same order as the request)

cursor.description: Last activity ID (use as offset for next request)

post.description: Invites one or more users to the system via email. Can be used to onboard system level users or admins.

rejectedIds.description: Identifiers of items that could not be processed

rejectionReasons.description: Map of reason code to rejected item IDs

delete.description: Deletes one or more users from the system based on a list of IDs. This also removes the user's memberships (cascade) and sets references to the user to null where applicable.

rejectedIds.description: Identifiers of items that could not be processed

rejectionReasons.description: Map of reason code to rejected item IDs

put.description: Updates a user identified by ID.

post.description: Sends a newsletter to members of one or more specified organizations.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of tenants. System admin access required.

post.description: Creates a new tenant. System admin access required.
Creates a new tenant for the authenticated user. Only allowed if the user has no existing tenant memberships.

put.description: Updates a tenant by ID. System admin access required.

get.description: Returns all domains belonging to a tenant, including verification tokens. System admin access required.

post.summary: Add a domain to a tenant
post.description: Adds a new domain to a tenant. The domain starts unverified. System admin access required.

delete.description: Removes a domain from a tenant. System admin access required.

get.description: Returns a single domain including its verification token for DNS TXT setup. System admin access required.

post.description: Looks up DNS TXT records for the domain to verify ownership. Checks for a _cella-verification.<domain> TXT record matching the verification token.
Submits a new request to the system. Supported types include contact form, newsletter signup, and waitlist entry.

example.message: Amitto sapiente antiquus temptatio verus mollitia.

get.description: Returns a list of submitted requests across all types: contact form, newsletter, and waitlist.

items.message: Cognomen deprimo compono defungo vix ullam abeo adimpleo tergo tardus.

delete.description: Deletes one or more requests from the system by their IDs.

rejectedIds.description: Identifiers of items that could not be processed

rejectionReasons.description: Map of reason code to rejected item IDs

get.description:
Returns basic count metrics for entity types such as users and organizations.
      This endpoint is public and uses a 1 minute in memory cache for performance.
Returns a list of users.

items.description: Base user schema with essential fields for identification and display.

get.description: Retrieves a user by ID. The requesting user must share at least one organization membership. Pass ?slug=true to resolve by slug instead.

schema.description: Base user schema with essential fields for identification and display.

get.description: Returns a context-scoped HMAC-signed token for a specific entity type. The token proves the user has update permission and can be verified by the Yjs relay worker without a backend callback.

post.description: Creates one or more new organizations within a tenant.

items.description: The main channel entity is an organization.

example.name: Spencer, Pouros and Bergnaum
example.welcomeText: Welcome to Spencer, Pouros and Bergnaum!

rejectedIds.description: Identifiers of items that could not be processed

rejectionReasons.description: Map of reason code to rejected item IDs

delete.description: Deletes one or more organizations by ID within a tenant.

rejectedIds.description: Identifiers of items that could not be processed

rejectionReasons.description: Map of reason code to rejected item IDs

get.description: Returns a list of organizations.

items.name: Wiegand, Harber and Stark-Abshire
items.welcomeText: Welcome to Wiegand, Harber and Stark-Abshire!

get.description: Retrieves an organization by ID within a tenant. Pass ?slug=true to resolve by slug instead.

example.name: Spencer, Pouros and Bergnaum
example.welcomeText: Welcome to Spencer, Pouros and Bergnaum!

put.description: Updates an organization within a tenant.

example.name: Spencer, Pouros and Bergnaum
example.welcomeText: Welcome to Spencer, Pouros and Bergnaum!

post.description: Model Context Protocol (JSON-RPC 2.0) endpoint. Exposes the workspace-scoped server tool registry to MCP clients (initialize, tools/list, tools/call).

get.description: Returns a paginated list of attachments for the organization.

post.description: Registers one or more new attachments after client side upload. Includes metadata like name, type, and linked entity.

rejectedIds.description: Identifiers of items that could not be processed

rejectionReasons.description: Map of reason code to rejected item IDs

rejectedIds.description: Identifiers of items that could not be processed

rejectionReasons.description: Map of reason code to rejected item IDs

delete.description: Deletes one or more attachment records by ID. This does not delete the underlying file in storage.

rejectedIds.description: Identifiers of items that could not be processed

rejectionReasons.description: Map of reason code to rejected item IDs

get.description: Generates and returns a presigned URL for accessing a private attachment file in S3. Public files should use the public CDN URL directly. Requires organization context.
Returns a single attachment by ID. Served from the CDC-invalidated entity detail cache.

put.description: Updates metadata of an attachment, such as its name or associated entity.

post.description: Creates one or more memberships, inviting users (existing or new) to a channel entity such as an organization.

rejectedIds.description: Identifiers of items that could not be processed

rejectionReasons.description: Map of reason code to rejected item IDs

delete.description: Deletes one or more memberships by ID. This removes the membership but does not delete the associated user(s).

rejectedIds.description: Identifiers of items that could not be processed

rejectionReasons.description: Map of reason code to rejected item IDs

put.description: Updates the membership metadata, such as role, muted, or archived status.

post.description: Accepting activates the associated membership. Rejecting simply removes the invitation token.

get.description: Retrieves members (users) of a channel entity by ID, including their associated membership data.

items.description: Base user schema with essential fields for identification and display.

get.description: Returns pending memberships for a channel entity, identified by ID. This does not include pending invitations for non-existing users.

post.description: Records that the current user has viewed one or more product entities. Deduplicates against existing records. Updates entity view counts for newly seen entities.

entityIds.description: Entity IDs the user has viewed since last batch

entityType.description: Entity type for all IDs in this batch

newCount.description: Number of entities newly marked as seen (deduped)
